title: 10 possible side effects of Hydrastis canadensis (Goldenseal) if used improperly
intro: Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, has been used in herbal medicine for centuries, but improper use can lead to several health issues. If taken in large amounts, it can cause digestive problems such as stomach cramps, nausea, and vomiting. Prolonged use or high doses can lead to more severe consequences, including elevated blood pressure, irregular heartbeat, and even muscle spasms. Additionally, goldenseal can interact with various medications, such as blood thinners and antidepressants, leading to dangerous adverse effects. Pregnant women should avoid goldenseal altogether, as it may stimulate uterine contractions and lead to miscarriage. Therefore, it is crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen, including the use of Hydrastis canadensis.
